item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly yeah, you aren't wrong at all. When i get around to a rewrite, i'll do the concept justice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While "Jersey Devil because Sarkicism" isn't a bad concept, the execution is very predictable and longer than it needs to be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hi, author and biology student here to say that this is just pants-on-head crazy levels of pedantic.

Chiropteran wings require extremely low body mass/wing area to be functional (though all wings do). They're also basically gigantic hands webbed between all the phalanges and also from the pinky to the ankle. They would also need to be, like 10 meters wide tip to tip.

This was the intended imagery.

Bat wings imply quite a lot about the remainder of the creature's anatomy, like it has short, near vestigial legs.

No, it just implies that it has bat wings, not that it is a bat.

The fact that you state "bipedal hircine creatures that exhibit numerous deviations from non-anomalous goats" is a bit ridiculous, as goats aren't bipedal in the first place.

That is like… the entire point of stating their bipedalism. It isn't normal for goats to be bipedal, which is why I specified as such.

Personally, bats are awesome and if you're going to use their physiology in an SCP article, use it right.

Lol ok bud, sure thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Bats are one of my favorite creatures, the recent bad publicity they've gotten reminded me of this.

So yeah. Chiropteran wings require extremely low body mass/wing area to be functional (though all wings do). They're also basically gigantic hands webbed between all the phalanges and also from the pinky to the ankle. They would also need to be, like 10 meters wide tip to tip. Bat wings imply quite a lot about the remainder of the creature's anatomy, like it has short, near vestigial legs.

This leads right into another, separate issue. The fact that you state "bipedal hircine creatures that exhibit numerous deviations from non-anomalous goats" is a bit ridiculous, as goats aren't bipedal in the first place.

All in all, you use scientific terminology to elevate the clinical tone of the article, but you use it wrong. To someone who understands (or looks up) what those words mean, the creature goes from scary to comical, or just plain unbelievable. Personally, bats are awesome and if you're going to use their physiology in an SCP article, use it right.

@ edna, SCP articles are supposed to be written by scientists. Asking that they use the correct terminology, or qualify it, is not pretentious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh yeah. I'm all about grammatical correctness, but some people take it a bit far.

I was just going to abstain from voting. But now I'm gonna +1 just out of spite.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So I'm sitting down to write a Parawatch about the jersey devil and I come to find you already did it. That said, +1 this is dope.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You do realise that, while they may be fictional, they are being written about in the context of a scientific document, right? Where using detailed information is expected, rather than simply writing "mythical demon wings"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As I said on the IRC, this is a pretentious as fuck reason to downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
We talked a bit on IRC.

I read it all, but -1 for the following:

with functional chiropteran wings protruding from the shoulder blades

You're describing mythical demon wings, not bat wings.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wouldn’t say it’s an article with a particularly powerful ending, but one thing it does do is stay consistent. Personally, I shivered when reading the ritual. +1

item.title: Not very cash money of you
item.content:encoded:
Nederbird, I'm cool with discussing biological vocabulary or whatever, but I'm not cool with people (incorrectly) changing words on my page without my permission.

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hircine
vs.
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Caprine

Just saying…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeahh looking back, I do wish I'd condensed some stuff and spent more time coming up with a more impactful climax. Hindsight is 20/20. I'll leave this up for the time being, but I might pull it for a rewrite after I have a few more published skips.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I dunno. The prose is good and there are some interesting parts, but overall I felt that the concept was milked for more than it's worth. The length takes a lot of the punch out of it for me, and the ending didn't feel especially rewarding.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm very pleased with this. It succeeds on three counts: it accurately represents the initial inspiration, connects it to pre existing concepts within the canon, and then combines those two ideas to create a unique identity. +1

item.title: Addendum 4059-4
item.content:encoded:
Added a short addendum at the end to replace my previous attempt at subtlety that felt rather cheap and exposition-y.

Begone worthless additions!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Happy to help! I'm glad you included the Blue Hole and gave it mysterious significance. Also, I really liked the ritual.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've been hoping for a Jersey Devil SCP for a while now. I was not disappointed, well done. +1

item.title: The Jersey Devil
item.content:encoded:
I was born 13th child, 'neath the 13th moon.
Spit out all hungry, and born anew
Daddy drag me to the river, tie me in rocks
Throw me in where its deep and wide
I go down, I don't die

This here is my first SCP. Always liked urban legend inspired articles, so I figured I'd try my hand at writing one myself.

Special thanks to:

  • Metaphysician for giving me tons of invaluable advice at every step, and for crafting the absolutely glorious Sarkicism Hub
  • Uncle Nicolini for the great criticisms (and free pony)
  • dankaar for getting back to me within an hour when I asked for a last minute once-over
  • MathBrush and Crow-Cat for helping me fine tune the idea in the first place
  • Mortos for helping me trim this thing down a ton.
+ Image Attribution
- Image Attribution

https://pxhere.com/en/photo/1210366
Image released into the public domain via CC0.
